articles.headline: CBI Uncovers NAAC Rating Bribery Scandal, Chairman Among Seven Arrested
articles.explainer:
• The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has arrested the chairman and six members of the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) in connection with a major bribery case.
• The arrests follow an extensive investigation into allegations of accepting monetary inducements for granting favorable accreditation ratings to educational institutions.
• This scandal raises significant concerns about the integrity of the higher education accreditation process and the quality assurance mechanisms in India.
• The Ministry of Education has initiated an internal inquiry and promised stringent action to restore faith in the accreditation system.
• Experts call for a complete overhaul of NAAC's operational framework to enhance transparency and accountability.
gsSubTopics: Governance
Transparency & Accountability
Ethics in Public Administration
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education
prelimsPoints: NAAC (National Assessment and Accreditation Council) is an autonomous body under UGC.
It assesses and accredits higher education institutions in India.
CBI (Central Bureau of Investigation) is the premier investigative agency of India.
The case highlights issues of integrity in educational governance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of corruption in regulatory bodies like NAAC on India's higher education system.
Analyze the role of institutional checks and balances in preventing such scandals.
Suggest reforms to enhance transparency and accountability in autonomous accreditation agencies.
Examine the ethical dilemmas faced by public officials in governance and the importance of integrity.
articles.imageDescription: CBI logo with a blurred background of a university building.
articles.trivia: NAAC was established in 1994 as an autonomous institution of the University Grants Commission (UGC) of India.

articles.headline: Union Budget 2025-26: Experts Laud Focus on Infrastructure and Green Growth
articles.explainer:
• Following its presentation yesterday, financial experts and industry leaders have largely welcomed the Union Budget 2025-26 for its emphasis on capital expenditure and sustainable development.
• The budget allocates significant funds towards boosting national infrastructure projects, including roads, railways, and urban development.
• Special impetus has been given to green technologies, renewable energy, and climate-resilient agriculture, aligning with India's net-zero commitments.
• Critics, however, point to the need for more direct relief measures for the informal sector and a clearer roadmap for job creation.
• The government aims to achieve a fiscal deficit target of 4.5% of GDP by FY2026, as outlined in the budget.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Union Budget is presented annually by the Finance Minister.
Fiscal deficit is the difference between total revenue and total expenditure of the government.
Capital expenditure contributes to asset creation and long-term growth.
India's net-zero target is by 2070.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential impact of increased capital expenditure on India's economic growth and employment generation.
Evaluate the strategies outlined in the budget for promoting green growth and achieving climate targets.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in balancing fiscal consolidation with developmental goals, referencing the 2025-26 budget.
Examine the criticisms regarding the budget's approach to the informal sector and job creation.
articles.imageDescription: Infographic showing key budget allocations for infrastructure and green initiatives.
articles.trivia: The first Union Budget of independent India was presented on 26 November 1947 by R. K. Shanmukham Chetty.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Right to Privacy in Landmark Data Sharing Verdict
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a pivotal judgment, reinforcing the fundamental Right to Privacy in a case concerning the sharing of personal data by private entities.
• The ruling emphasizes that individual consent is paramount for data collection and sharing, particularly sensitive personal information.
• The court directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law that aligns with global best practices and constitutional principles.
• This verdict has significant implications for technology companies, financial institutions, and all entities handling citizen data.
• It builds upon the Puttaswamy judgment, further strengthening India's jurisprudence on privacy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Fundamental Rights
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Role of Supreme Court in protecting fundamental rights
prelimsPoints: Right to Privacy was declared a Fundamental Right under Article 21 by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy case (2017).
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is the current framework for data protection in India.
The Supreme Court is the highest judicial body in India, guardian of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the Right to Privacy in India's legal landscape, with specific reference to recent Supreme Court judgments.
Discuss the challenges in implementing a robust data protection framework in a digitally evolving society like India.
Examine the responsibilities of private entities and the government in safeguarding citizens' personal data.
Evaluate the balance between national security interests and individual privacy rights in the digital age.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building with a digital overlay representing data privacy.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' is an aspect of the Right to Privacy, allowing individuals to request removal of their personal data from public platforms.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Strengthening Indo-Pacific Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The 14th India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming both nations' commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on enhancing security cooperation, economic partnership, and technological collaboration, particularly in critical and emerging technologies.
• Both sides condemned recent destabilizing actions in the region and reiterated adherence to international law, including UNCLOS.
• Key outcomes include new initiatives in supply chain resilience and joint maritime exercises.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of interests between India and Japan in regional and global affairs.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) along with the US and Australia.
UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea) governs maritime activities.
The Indo-Pacific region is critical for global trade and security.
India and Japan have a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan strategic partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and Japan, including economic, security, and technological aspects.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its alliances in the Indo-Pacific region.
Examine how adherence to international law, like UNCLOS, contributes to regional stability.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: India is the largest recipient of Japan's Official Development Assistance (ODA).

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched its latest Earth Observation Satellite, EOS-07, from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota.
• Carried aboard the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-C56), EOS-07 will provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including agriculture, forestry, and disaster management.
• This mission further bolsters India's capabilities in remote sensing and contributes to global efforts in climate monitoring.
• The satellite is equipped with advanced sensors for multi-spectral imaging and data collection.
• ISRO Chairman highlighted the indigenous development of all components, emphasizing India's growing self-reliance in space technology.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle.
Earth Observation Satellites are used for monitoring Earth's environment, resources, and climate.
Satish Dhawan Space Centre is ISRO's primary spaceport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's national development and security.
Analyze ISRO's role in promoting indigenization of technology and its impact on India's global standing in space.
Examine the applications of remote sensing technology in fields like disaster management, urban planning, and resource mapping.
Evaluate the challenges and future prospects of India's space program.
articles.imageDescription: PSLV rocket launching from Sriharikota with smoke trails.
articles.trivia: ISRO holds the record for launching 104 satellites in a single mission in February 2017, a world record at the time.

articles.headline: Government Unveils Detailed Guidelines for National Green Hydrogen Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy today released the comprehensive operational guidelines for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, signaling a major push for clean energy.
• The guidelines outline incentives for green hydrogen production, manufacturing of electrolyzers, and development of green hydrogen hubs.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Focus areas include research and development, skill development, and creating a robust regulatory framework.
• This initiative is crucial for India to achieve its climate targets and enhance energy security.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Green Hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for India to become a major producer and exporter of green hydrogen.
Electrolyzers are devices used to split water into hydrogen and oxygen.
India targets achieving Net Zero emissions by 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the potential economic and environmental benefits of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Examine the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and the role of government incentives.
Compare green hydrogen with other clean energy sources and its prospects in India's energy mix.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the production process of green hydrogen using renewable energy sources.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Approves Major Indigenous Procurement for Naval Fleet Modernization
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, has approved several proposals for indigenous procurement aimed at modernizing the Indian Naval fleet.
• The approvals include the acquisition of advanced warships, submarines, and specialized reconnaissance aircraft, all to be manufactured in India.
• This move aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative, significantly boosting domestic defence manufacturing capabilities and reducing import dependence.
• The projects are expected to generate substantial employment and foster technological advancements in the defence sector.
• Emphasis was placed on cutting-edge technology and interoperability with existing naval assets.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body in the Defence Ministry for procurement.
Atmanirbhar Bharat (Self-Reliant India) initiative promotes domestic manufacturing.
Indian Navy is one of the three branches of the Indian Armed Forces.
Indigenous defence production reduces reliance on foreign suppliers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic rationale behind India's push for indigenous defence manufacturing and its implications for national security.
Analyze the role of initiatives like 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in transforming India's defence industrial base.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in achieving self-reliance in advanced defence technologies for the naval sector.
Evaluate the economic benefits and job creation potential of large-scale indigenous defence procurement.
articles.imageDescription: A modern Indian Navy warship sailing at sea, symbolizing naval modernization.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defence equipment, a trend the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative aims to reverse.

articles.headline: PM-SHRI Schools Initiative Expands to Cover More Districts, Boosting Quality Education
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced the expansion of the PM-SHRI (PM Schools for Rising India) scheme to include additional districts across the country.
• The initiative aims to upgrade and develop over 14,500 schools as model schools, incorporating NEP 2020 principles and providing quality education.
• These schools will offer modern infrastructure, smart classrooms, and holistic learning experiences, focusing on critical thinking and experiential learning.
• The expansion seeks to ensure equitable access to high-quality education, particularly in underserved regions.
• PM-SHRI schools are envisioned as exemplars that will mentor other schools in their vicinity.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: PM-SHRI stands for PM Schools for Rising India.
The scheme aims to upgrade existing schools to model schools.
It aligns with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
The Ministry of Education is the nodal ministry for PM-SHRI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and potential impact of the PM-SHRI scheme on transforming the landscape of school education in India.
Discuss how the PM-SHRI initiative aligns with the vision and recommendations of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing such a large-scale educational reform and ensuring equitable access to quality education.
Examine the role of technology and modern infrastructure in enhancing learning outcomes in PM-SHRI schools.
articles.imageDescription: Children studying in a modern classroom setting with digital learning tools.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India.

articles.headline: RBI MPC Maintains Repo Rate, Prioritizes Inflation Control Amid Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%, extending its pause for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects the MPC's continued cautious stance, prioritizing inflation control amidst persistent global supply chain disruptions and volatile commodity prices.
• RBI Governor stated that the focus remains on withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest that future rate cuts are unlikely until inflation shows a definitive downward trend and settles within the comfort zone.
• The move aims to maintain financial stability and anchor inflation expectations.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Monetary policy
Inflation
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for India's economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Examine the impact of global economic uncertainties on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in managing inflation and ensuring financial stability.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic indicators in the background.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Urgent Need for Robust Urban Flood Management in Major Cities
articles.explainer:
• A new report released by the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) underscores the critical need for comprehensive urban flood management strategies across major Indian cities.
• The report points to rapid urbanization, inadequate drainage systems, and climate change-induced extreme rainfall events as primary drivers of increased flood vulnerability.
• It recommends integrated water management plans, early warning systems, and community-based disaster preparedness programs.
• Focus areas include rejuvenating natural drainage systems, permeable infrastructure, and strict enforcement of building codes.
• The findings are crucial for policymakers to develop resilient urban infrastructure and mitigate future flood risks.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: NDMA (National Disaster Management Authority) is the apex body for disaster management in India.
Urban flooding is caused by intense rainfall exceeding the capacity of drainage systems.
Climate change leads to an increase in extreme weather events.
Smart city initiatives often include resilient infrastructure planning.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifactorial causes of urban flooding in Indian cities and its socio-economic consequences.
Discuss the key recommendations of the NDMA report for effective urban flood management, including structural and non-structural measures.
Examine the role of urban planning, governance, and community participation in building flood-resilient cities.
Evaluate how climate change exacerbates urban flood risks and the adaptive strategies required.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a flooded urban area with submerged roads and buildings.
articles.trivia: Mumbai's Mithi River, once a clean waterway, became a major factor in the 2005 Mumbai floods due to encroachment and pollution.

articles.headline: Ministry of Culture Launches Pan-India Digital Archiving Project for Cultural Heritage
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture today launched an ambitious pan-India project for the digital archiving and preservation of India's vast cultural heritage.
• The initiative aims to digitize ancient manuscripts, historical documents, artworks, and intangible cultural expressions, making them accessible to researchers and the public worldwide.
• This project leverages advanced imaging and data management technologies to prevent decay and ensure the longevity of invaluable cultural assets.
• Collaboration with academic institutions, museums, and local communities will be a cornerstone of the project.
• It is expected to significantly boost cultural tourism and academic research into India's rich past.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of technology in preserving heritage.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Culture is responsible for the preservation and promotion of Indian art and culture.
Intangible Cultural Heritage includes oral traditions, performing arts, social practices, rituals, festive events, etc.
Digital archiving helps in preserving fragile historical documents.
UNESCO also recognizes and promotes World Heritage Sites and Intangible Cultural Heritage.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of digital archiving in preserving India's diverse cultural heritage for future generations.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in leveraging technology for cultural preservation and accessibility.
Examine how such initiatives can contribute to cultural tourism, research, and national identity.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and community engagement in large-scale heritage preservation projects.
articles.imageDescription: An ancient Indian manuscript being carefully digitized by a researcher.
articles.trivia: The 'Memory of the World Programme' by UNESCO aims to preserve and promote documentary heritage.

articles.headline: India Proposes New Framework for Global AI Governance at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India today presented a comprehensive framework for global Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance at a United Nations forum, advocating for ethical and inclusive AI development.
• The proposed framework emphasizes transparency, accountability, data privacy, and the prevention of AI misuse in critical sectors.
• India highlighted the need for international cooperation to bridge the digital divide and ensure equitable access to AI benefits for developing nations.
• The initiative reflects India's growing leadership in the global technology landscape and its commitment to responsible AI innovation.
• Discussions also touched upon the potential of AI for sustainable development goals.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the simulation of human intelligence in machines.
The United Nations is an intergovernmental organization aiming to maintain international peace and security.
India is a leading nation in IT and software development.
Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a collection of 17 interlinked global goals designed to be a 'blueprint to achieve a better and more sustainable future for all'.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's vision for a global AI governance framework and its significance in the context of emerging technologies.
Analyze the ethical considerations and potential risks associated with the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence.
Examine the challenges in achieving international consensus on AI governance and the role of developing nations.
Evaluate how AI can contribute to achieving Sustainable Development Goals, while addressing the digital divide.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of AI algorithms with a globe in the background, symbolizing global governance.
articles.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956.

articles.headline: Health Ministry Launches Nationwide Campaign Against Non-Communicable Diseases
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare today inaugurated a comprehensive nationwide campaign aimed at raising awareness and prevention of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The campaign focuses on promoting healthy lifestyles, regular health screenings, and early detection of conditions like diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular diseases.
• It involves extensive outreach programs, public service announcements, and collaboration with local health bodies and NGOs.
• NCDs are a growing public health challenge in India, accounting for a significant burden of disease and mortality.
• The initiative is part of India's commitment to achieving UN Sustainable Development Goal 3, which focuses on good health and well-being.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NCDs are chronic diseases that are not transmitted from person to person (e.g., heart disease, cancer, diabetes).
SDG 3 aims to 'Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages'.
Preventive healthcare focuses on preventing diseases rather than treating them.
Ayushman Bharat is a flagship scheme for universal health coverage in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the increasing burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India and its socio-economic implications.
Discuss the multi-pronged approach adopted by the government's nationwide campaign to combat NCDs.
Examine the role of lifestyle changes, public awareness, and early detection in controlling the spread of NCDs.
Evaluate India's progress and challenges in achieving health-related Sustainable Development Goals, particularly concerning NCDs.
articles.imageDescription: A diverse group of people engaging in healthy activities like yoga and walking, symbolizing preventive health.
articles.trivia: Globally, NCDs kill 41 million people each year, equivalent to 74% of all deaths.

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic All-Weather Tunnel in Himalayas
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) today announced the completion of a strategically vital all-weather tunnel in the challenging Himalayan terrain, significantly enhancing connectivity to remote border areas.
• This engineering marvel will ensure year-round access, crucial for military logistics, civilian movement, and economic development in the region.
• The tunnel reduces travel time and provides a safer alternative to avalanche-prone routes, especially during harsh winter months.
• It is part of India's broader strategy to upgrade border infrastructure and strengthen national security along sensitive frontiers.
• The project utilized advanced tunneling technology and overcame severe geological and climatic challenges.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: BRO (Border Roads Organisation) constructs and maintains road networks in border areas of India.
Strategic tunnels are crucial for all-weather connectivity in mountainous regions.
The Himalayas are a major mountain range in Asia.
Improved border infrastructure aids both military and civilian movement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing robust infrastructure in India's border regions, with a focus on the Himalayas.
Analyze the role of the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) in enhancing national security and economic development in challenging terrains.
Examine the engineering challenges encountered in constructing tunnels in the Himalayas and the technological solutions employed.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of improved connectivity on the lives of people residing in remote border areas.
articles.imageDescription: An entrance to a newly constructed tunnel in a snowy mountainous region.
articles.trivia: The Atal Tunnel, also constructed by BRO, is the world's longest highway single-tube tunnel above 10,000 feet (3,000 m).

articles.headline: Government to Further Promote Millets and Organic Farming Through New Agricultural Reforms
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare today announced a new set of agricultural reforms aimed at boosting the cultivation of millets and promoting organic farming practices.
• These reforms include enhanced financial incentives, dedicated research and development, and market linkages for millet farmers.
• The focus on organic farming aims to reduce chemical fertilizer use, improve soil health, and cater to growing demand for sustainable produce.
• The initiatives are part of a broader strategy to diversify crop production, ensure food security, and enhance farmers' income.
• India is a major producer of millets, and the move aligns with the 'International Year of Millets' momentum.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Land reforms in India.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Millets are a group of highly nutritious and drought-resistant coarse grains (e.g., Jowar, Bajra, Ragi).
2023 was designated as the 'International Year of Millets' by the UN.
Organic farming avoids synthetic fertilizers and pesticides.
The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare is responsible for agricultural policies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of millets in ensuring India's food security, nutritional needs, and climate resilience.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of promoting organic farming practices on a large scale in India.
Examine how government policies and market interventions can support farmers in transitioning to millet cultivation and organic farming.
Evaluate the potential of agricultural diversification, including millets, in enhancing farmers' income and promoting sustainable agriculture.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting a field of millets under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: India is the largest producer of millets in the world, accounting for 41% of global production.

articles.headline: Editorial: The Ethics of Academic Governance - Lessons from the NAAC Scandal
articles.explainer:
• The recent NAAC bribery scandal has cast a long shadow on the integrity of academic governance in India, demanding immediate and fundamental reforms.
• This incident highlights systemic vulnerabilities in accreditation processes, where financial incentives can compromise academic standards and public trust.
• The editorial calls for a complete overhaul of NAAC's structure, emphasizing greater autonomy, transparency, and accountability mechanisms.
• It advocates for stricter ethical codes for officials involved in accreditation and robust whistleblower protection policies.
• Beyond NAAC, the scandal serves as a stark reminder of the broader need to strengthen ethical foundations across all regulatory bodies in the education sector.
gsSubTopics: Governance
Transparency & Accountability
Ethics in Public Administration
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the ethical implications of the NAAC bribery scandal on the credibility of India's higher education system.
Suggest comprehensive reforms for enhancing transparency, accountability, and ethical conduct in autonomous regulatory bodies like NAAC.
Discuss the role of leadership and institutional culture in preventing corruption within academic governance structures.
Examine how a lack of integrity in accreditation processes can undermine the quality and standards of educational institutions.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel striking a block on a desk, symbolizing justice and accountability in governance.
articles.trivia: The concept of academic freedom is crucial for ensuring the integrity and quality of higher education institutions.

highlights: CBI arrests NAAC chairman and six members in a major bribery scandal, raising questions about academic governance integrity.
Union Budget 2025-26 receives praise for its strong focus on infrastructure development and green growth initiatives.
Supreme Court delivers landmark verdict, reinforcing the fundamental Right to Privacy in data sharing cases.
India and Japan conclude strategic dialogue, strengthening cooperation in Indo-Pacific security and economy.
ISRO successfully launches Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07, enhancing India's remote sensing capabilities.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent NAAC rating bribery case, consider the following statements:
1. NAAC is an autonomous institution under the Ministry of Education.
2. The primary role of NAAC is to provide financial grants to higher educational institutions.
3. The CBI is India's premier agency for investigating corruption and economic offenses.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NAAC (National Assessment and Accreditation Council) is indeed an autonomous body under the University Grants Commission (UGC), which functions under the Ministry of Education. Statement 2 is incorrect: The primary role of NAAC is to assess and accredit higher education institutions, not to provide financial grants. That is largely the role of the UGC. Statement 3 is correct: The CBI (Central Bureau of Investigation) is the main investigative agency for corruption cases in India.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements best describes 'Green Hydrogen' as discussed in the context of the National Green Hydrogen Mission?
(a) Hydrogen produced from natural gas with carbon capture and storage.
(b) Hydrogen produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
(c) Hydrogen produced from coal gasification.
(d) Hydrogen produced using nuclear energy.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Hydrogen produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Green Hydrogen specifically refers to hydrogen produced through the electrolysis of water, powered by renewable energy sources like solar or wind. This process generates no greenhouse gas emissions. Option (a) describes 'Blue Hydrogen', (c) describes 'Grey Hydrogen', and (d) could be 'Pink Hydrogen'.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC):
1. It is a statutory body established under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.
2. Its primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
3. The Governor of the Reserve Bank of India is the ex-officio Chairperson of the MPC.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The MPC is a statutory body formed under the RBI Act, 1934, with the primary objective of price stability while considering growth. The RBI Governor serves as its ex-officio Chairperson.

prelimsMocks.question:
The PM-SHRI scheme, recently expanded, is primarily associated with which of the following sectors?
(a) Rural housing development
(b) Healthcare infrastructure
(c) Quality school education
(d) Agricultural market reforms
prelimsMocks.explanation: PM-SHRI stands for PM Schools for Rising India. It is a centrally sponsored scheme aimed at upgrading and developing more than 14,500 schools across India as model schools, delivering quality education and showcasing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of 'Millets' as promoted by the government for agricultural reforms?
(a) Highly nutritious
(b) Drought-resistant
(c) Require extensive irrigation
(d) Contribute to food security
prelimsMocks.explanation: Millets are known for their resilience and ability to grow in arid or semi-arid conditions, making them drought-resistant and requiring less water compared to other staple crops like rice and wheat. Therefore, requiring extensive irrigation is not a characteristic of millets.

mainsMocks.question: The recent NAAC bribery scandal highlights systemic vulnerabilities in India's higher education governance. Critically analyze the ethical implications of such corruption and suggest comprehensive reforms to restore public trust and ensure quality assurance in educational accreditation. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Governance, Transparency & Accountability; GS4: Ethics in Public Administration
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the NAAC scandal and its ethical breach. Discuss implications: erosion of public trust, compromised academic standards, impact on quality of education. Propose reforms: structural overhaul of NAAC, enhanced transparency, stricter ethical codes, whistleblower protection, digital monitoring, and promoting a culture of integrity.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic importance of developing robust infrastructure in India's border regions, with a focus on the Himalayas. How does the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) contribute to both national security and socio-economic development in these challenging terrains? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas; Infrastructure: Roads; GS1: Important Geophysical phenomena
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by highlighting the geopolitical significance of border infrastructure, especially in the Himalayas. Discuss its role in national security (troop movement, logistics, surveillance) and socio-economic development (tourism, trade, access to services for locals). Detail BRO's role in constructing all-weather roads, tunnels, and bridges, overcoming geographical challenges. Conclude by emphasizing the dual benefit of BRO's work.

mainsMocks.question: India's push for global AI governance and the launch of the National Green Hydrogen Mission reflect its growing leadership in technology and sustainable development. Analyze how these initiatives contribute to India's strategic autonomy and its role on the global stage. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: International Relations, Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests; GS3: Science and Technology, Environmental pollution and degradation, Infrastructure: Energy
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's proactive stance on AI governance (ethical, inclusive AI) and Green Hydrogen (energy security, climate targets). For AI, discuss its contribution to shaping global norms, bridging the digital divide, and ensuring responsible tech use. For Green Hydrogen, explain its role in energy transition, reducing fossil fuel dependence, and creating new economic opportunities. Connect both to India's 'strategic autonomy' and 'global leadership' narrative, demonstrating how these initiatives enhance its influence and self-reliance.

quickBytes.text: NAAC is an autonomous body under UGC for assessing and accrediting higher education institutions.
Green Hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the policy interest rates (e.g., Repo Rate) in India.
PM-SHRI scheme aims to upgrade over 14,500 schools as model schools, aligning with NEP 2020.
Millets are highly nutritious, drought-resistant grains, promoted for food security and farmer income.
The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) constructs and maintains roads in border areas of India.
The Right to Privacy was declared a Fundamental Right under Article 21 by the Supreme Court.
ISRO's PSLV is known as the 'workhorse' launch vehicle for Earth Observation Satellites.
India and Japan are key partners in the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) grouping.
NCDs (Non-Communicable Diseases) are chronic diseases like diabetes, heart disease, and cancer.

GS1.headline: Report Highlights Urgent Need for Robust Urban Flood Management in Major Cities
GS1.explainer:
• A new report released by the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) underscores the critical need for comprehensive urban flood management strategies across major Indian cities.
• The report points to rapid urbanization, inadequate drainage systems, and climate change-induced extreme rainfall events as primary drivers of increased flood vulnerability.
• It recommends integrated water management plans, early warning systems, and community-based disaster preparedness programs.
• Focus areas include rejuvenating natural drainage systems, permeable infrastructure, and strict enforcement of building codes.
• The findings are crucial for policymakers to develop resilient urban infrastructure and mitigate future flood risks.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: NDMA (National Disaster Management Authority) is the apex body for disaster management in India.
Urban flooding is caused by intense rainfall exceeding the capacity of drainage systems.
Climate change leads to an increase in extreme weather events.
Smart city initiatives often include resilient infrastructure planning.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifactorial causes of urban flooding in Indian cities and its socio-economic consequences.
Discuss the key recommendations of the NDMA report for effective urban flood management, including structural and non-structural measures.
Examine the role of urban planning, governance, and community participation in building flood-resilient cities.
Evaluate how climate change exacerbates urban flood risks and the adaptive strategies required.
GS1.imageDescription: An aerial view of a flooded urban area with submerged roads and buildings.
GS1.trivia: Mumbai's Mithi River, once a clean waterway, became a major factor in the 2005 Mumbai floods due to encroachment and pollution.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Culture Launches Pan-India Digital Archiving Project for Cultural Heritage
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture today launched an ambitious pan-India project for the digital archiving and preservation of India's vast cultural heritage.
• The initiative aims to digitize ancient manuscripts, historical documents, artworks, and intangible cultural expressions, making them accessible to researchers and the public worldwide.
• This project leverages advanced imaging and data management technologies to prevent decay and ensure the longevity of invaluable cultural assets.
• Collaboration with academic institutions, museums, and local communities will be a cornerstone of the project.
• It is expected to significantly boost cultural tourism and academic research into India's rich past.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of technology in preserving heritage.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Culture is responsible for the preservation and promotion of Indian art and culture.
Intangible Cultural Heritage includes oral traditions, performing arts, social practices, rituals, festive events, etc.
Digital archiving helps in preserving fragile historical documents.
UNESCO also recognizes and promotes World Heritage Sites and Intangible Cultural Heritage.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of digital archiving in preserving India's diverse cultural heritage for future generations.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in leveraging technology for cultural preservation and accessibility.
Examine how such initiatives can contribute to cultural tourism, research, and national identity.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and community engagement in large-scale heritage preservation projects.
GS1.imageDescription: An ancient Indian manuscript being carefully digitized by a researcher.
GS1.trivia: The 'Memory of the World Programme' by UNESCO aims to preserve and promote documentary heritage.

GS2.headline: CBI Uncovers NAAC Rating Bribery Scandal, Chairman Among Seven Arrested
GS2.explainer:
• The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has arrested the chairman and six members of the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) in connection with a major bribery case.
• The arrests follow an extensive investigation into allegations of accepting monetary inducements for granting favorable accreditation ratings to educational institutions.
• This scandal raises significant concerns about the integrity of the higher education accreditation process and the quality assurance mechanisms in India.
• The Ministry of Education has initiated an internal inquiry and promised stringent action to restore faith in the accreditation system.
• Experts call for a complete overhaul of NAAC's operational framework to enhance transparency and accountability.
gsSubTopics: Governance
Transparency & Accountability
Ethics in Public Administration
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education
prelimsPoints: NAAC (National Assessment and Accreditation Council) is an autonomous body under UGC.
It assesses and accredits higher education institutions in India.
CBI (Central Bureau of Investigation) is the premier investigative agency of India.
The case highlights issues of integrity in educational governance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of corruption in regulatory bodies like NAAC on India's higher education system.
Analyze the role of institutional checks and balances in preventing such scandals.
Suggest reforms to enhance transparency and accountability in autonomous accreditation agencies.
Examine the ethical dilemmas faced by public officials in governance and the importance of integrity.
GS2.imageDescription: CBI logo with a blurred background of a university building.
GS2.trivia: NAAC was established in 1994 as an autonomous institution of the University Grants Commission (UGC) of India.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Right to Privacy in Landmark Data Sharing Verdict
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a pivotal judgment, reinforcing the fundamental Right to Privacy in a case concerning the sharing of personal data by private entities.
• The ruling emphasizes that individual consent is paramount for data collection and sharing, particularly sensitive personal information.
• The court directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law that aligns with global best practices and constitutional principles.
• This verdict has significant implications for technology companies, financial institutions, and all entities handling citizen data.
• It builds upon the Puttaswamy judgment, further strengthening India's jurisprudence on privacy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Fundamental Rights
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Role of Supreme Court in protecting fundamental rights
prelimsPoints: Right to Privacy was declared a Fundamental Right under Article 21 by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy case (2017).
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is the current framework for data protection in India.
The Supreme Court is the highest judicial body in India, guardian of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the Right to Privacy in India's legal landscape, with specific reference to recent Supreme Court judgments.
Discuss the challenges in implementing a robust data protection framework in a digitally evolving society like India.
Examine the responsibilities of private entities and the government in safeguarding citizens' personal data.
Evaluate the balance between national security interests and individual privacy rights in the digital age.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building with a digital overlay representing data privacy.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' is an aspect of the Right to Privacy, allowing individuals to request removal of their personal data from public platforms.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Strengthening Indo-Pacific Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The 14th India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming both nations' commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on enhancing security cooperation, economic partnership, and technological collaboration, particularly in critical and emerging technologies.
• Both sides condemned recent destabilizing actions in the region and reiterated adherence to international law, including UNCLOS.
• Key outcomes include new initiatives in supply chain resilience and joint maritime exercises.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of interests between India and Japan in regional and global affairs.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) along with the US and Australia.
UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea) governs maritime activities.
The Indo-Pacific region is critical for global trade and security.
India and Japan have a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan strategic partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and Japan, including economic, security, and technological aspects.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its alliances in the Indo-Pacific region.
Examine how adherence to international law, like UNCLOS, contributes to regional stability.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: India is the largest recipient of Japan's Official Development Assistance (ODA).

GS2.headline: PM-SHRI Schools Initiative Expands to Cover More Districts, Boosting Quality Education
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced the expansion of the PM-SHRI (PM Schools for Rising India) scheme to include additional districts across the country.
• The initiative aims to upgrade and develop over 14,500 schools as model schools, incorporating NEP 2020 principles and providing quality education.
• These schools will offer modern infrastructure, smart classrooms, and holistic learning experiences, focusing on critical thinking and experiential learning.
• The expansion seeks to ensure equitable access to high-quality education, particularly in underserved regions.
• PM-SHRI schools are envisioned as exemplars that will mentor other schools in their vicinity.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: PM-SHRI stands for PM Schools for Rising India.
The scheme aims to upgrade existing schools to model schools.
It aligns with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
The Ministry of Education is the nodal ministry for PM-SHRI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and potential impact of the PM-SHRI scheme on transforming the landscape of school education in India.
Discuss how the PM-SHRI initiative aligns with the vision and recommendations of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing such a large-scale educational reform and ensuring equitable access to quality education.
Examine the role of technology and modern infrastructure in enhancing learning outcomes in PM-SHRI schools.
GS2.imageDescription: Children studying in a modern classroom setting with digital learning tools.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India.

GS2.headline: India Proposes New Framework for Global AI Governance at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India today presented a comprehensive framework for global Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance at a United Nations forum, advocating for ethical and inclusive AI development.
• The proposed framework emphasizes transparency, accountability, data privacy, and the prevention of AI misuse in critical sectors.
• India highlighted the need for international cooperation to bridge the digital divide and ensure equitable access to AI benefits for developing nations.
• The initiative reflects India's growing leadership in the global technology landscape and its commitment to responsible AI innovation.
• Discussions also touched upon the potential of AI for sustainable development goals.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the simulation of human intelligence in machines.
The United Nations is an intergovernmental organization aiming to maintain international peace and security.
India is a leading nation in IT and software development.
Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a collection of 17 interlinked global goals designed to be a 'blueprint to achieve a better and more sustainable future for all'.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's vision for a global AI governance framework and its significance in the context of emerging technologies.
Analyze the ethical considerations and potential risks associated with the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence.
Examine the challenges in achieving international consensus on AI governance and the role of developing nations.
Evaluate how AI can contribute to achieving Sustainable Development Goals, while addressing the digital divide.
GS2.imageDescription: Abstract representation of AI algorithms with a globe in the background, symbolizing global governance.
GS2.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956.

GS2.headline: Health Ministry Launches Nationwide Campaign Against Non-Communicable Diseases
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare today inaugurated a comprehensive nationwide campaign aimed at raising awareness and prevention of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The campaign focuses on promoting healthy lifestyles, regular health screenings, and early detection of conditions like diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular diseases.
• It involves extensive outreach programs, public service announcements, and collaboration with local health bodies and NGOs.
• NCDs are a growing public health challenge in India, accounting for a significant burden of disease and mortality.
• The initiative is part of India's commitment to achieving UN Sustainable Development Goal 3, which focuses on good health and well-being.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NCDs are chronic diseases that are not transmitted from person to person (e.g., heart disease, cancer, diabetes).
SDG 3 aims to 'Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages'.
Preventive healthcare focuses on preventing diseases rather than treating them.
Ayushman Bharat is a flagship scheme for universal health coverage in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the increasing burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India and its socio-economic implications.
Discuss the multi-pronged approach adopted by the government's nationwide campaign to combat NCDs.
Examine the role of lifestyle changes, public awareness, and early detection in controlling the spread of NCDs.
Evaluate India's progress and challenges in achieving health-related Sustainable Development Goals, particularly concerning NCDs.
GS2.imageDescription: A diverse group of people engaging in healthy activities like yoga and walking, symbolizing preventive health.
GS2.trivia: Globally, NCDs kill 41 million people each year, equivalent to 74% of all deaths.

GS2.headline: Editorial: The Ethics of Academic Governance - Lessons from the NAAC Scandal
GS2.explainer:
• The recent NAAC bribery scandal has cast a long shadow on the integrity of academic governance in India, demanding immediate and fundamental reforms.
• This incident highlights systemic vulnerabilities in accreditation processes, where financial incentives can compromise academic standards and public trust.
• The editorial calls for a complete overhaul of NAAC's structure, emphasizing greater autonomy, transparency, and accountability mechanisms.
• It advocates for stricter ethical codes for officials involved in accreditation and robust whistleblower protection policies.
• Beyond NAAC, the scandal serves as a stark reminder of the broader need to strengthen ethical foundations across all regulatory bodies in the education sector.
gsSubTopics: Governance
Transparency & Accountability
Ethics in Public Administration
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the ethical implications of the NAAC bribery scandal on the credibility of India's higher education system.
Suggest comprehensive reforms for enhancing transparency, accountability, and ethical conduct in autonomous regulatory bodies like NAAC.
Discuss the role of leadership and institutional culture in preventing corruption within academic governance structures.
Examine how a lack of integrity in accreditation processes can undermine the quality and standards of educational institutions.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel striking a block on a desk, symbolizing justice and accountability in governance.
GS2.trivia: The concept of academic freedom is crucial for ensuring the integrity and quality of higher education institutions.

GS3.headline: Union Budget 2025-26: Experts Laud Focus on Infrastructure and Green Growth
GS3.explainer:
• Following its presentation yesterday, financial experts and industry leaders have largely welcomed the Union Budget 2025-26 for its emphasis on capital expenditure and sustainable development.
• The budget allocates significant funds towards boosting national infrastructure projects, including roads, railways, and urban development.
• Special impetus has been given to green technologies, renewable energy, and climate-resilient agriculture, aligning with India's net-zero commitments.
• Critics, however, point to the need for more direct relief measures for the informal sector and a clearer roadmap for job creation.
• The government aims to achieve a fiscal deficit target of 4.5% of GDP by FY2026, as outlined in the budget.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Union Budget is presented annually by the Finance Minister.
Fiscal deficit is the difference between total revenue and total expenditure of the government.
Capital expenditure contributes to asset creation and long-term growth.
India's net-zero target is by 2070.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential impact of increased capital expenditure on India's economic growth and employment generation.
Evaluate the strategies outlined in the budget for promoting green growth and achieving climate targets.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in balancing fiscal consolidation with developmental goals, referencing the 2025-26 budget.
Examine the criticisms regarding the budget's approach to the informal sector and job creation.
GS3.imageDescription: Infographic showing key budget allocations for infrastructure and green initiatives.
GS3.trivia: The first Union Budget of independent India was presented on 26 November 1947 by R. K. Shanmukham Chetty.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched its latest Earth Observation Satellite, EOS-07, from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota.
• Carried aboard the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-C56), EOS-07 will provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including agriculture, forestry, and disaster management.
• This mission further bolsters India's capabilities in remote sensing and contributes to global efforts in climate monitoring.
• The satellite is equipped with advanced sensors for multi-spectral imaging and data collection.
• ISRO Chairman highlighted the indigenous development of all components, emphasizing India's growing self-reliance in space technology.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle.
Earth Observation Satellites are used for monitoring Earth's environment, resources, and climate.
Satish Dhawan Space Centre is ISRO's primary spaceport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's national development and security.
Analyze ISRO's role in promoting indigenization of technology and its impact on India's global standing in space.
Examine the applications of remote sensing technology in fields like disaster management, urban planning, and resource mapping.
Evaluate the challenges and future prospects of India's space program.
GS3.imageDescription: PSLV rocket launching from Sriharikota with smoke trails.
GS3.trivia: ISRO holds the record for launching 104 satellites in a single mission in February 2017, a world record at the time.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Detailed Guidelines for National Green Hydrogen Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy today released the comprehensive operational guidelines for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, signaling a major push for clean energy.
• The guidelines outline incentives for green hydrogen production, manufacturing of electrolyzers, and development of green hydrogen hubs.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Focus areas include research and development, skill development, and creating a robust regulatory framework.
• This initiative is crucial for India to achieve its climate targets and enhance energy security.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Green Hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for India to become a major producer and exporter of green hydrogen.
Electrolyzers are devices used to split water into hydrogen and oxygen.
India targets achieving Net Zero emissions by 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the potential economic and environmental benefits of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Examine the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and the role of government incentives.
Compare green hydrogen with other clean energy sources and its prospects in India's energy mix.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the production process of green hydrogen using renewable energy sources.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: Defence Ministry Approves Major Indigenous Procurement for Naval Fleet Modernization
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, has approved several proposals for indigenous procurement aimed at modernizing the Indian Naval fleet.
• The approvals include the acquisition of advanced warships, submarines, and specialized reconnaissance aircraft, all to be manufactured in India.
• This move aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative, significantly boosting domestic defence manufacturing capabilities and reducing import dependence.
• The projects are expected to generate substantial employment and foster technological advancements in the defence sector.
• Emphasis was placed on cutting-edge technology and interoperability with existing naval assets.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body in the Defence Ministry for procurement.
Atmanirbhar Bharat (Self-Reliant India) initiative promotes domestic manufacturing.
Indian Navy is one of the three branches of the Indian Armed Forces.
Indigenous defence production reduces reliance on foreign suppliers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic rationale behind India's push for indigenous defence manufacturing and its implications for national security.
Analyze the role of initiatives like 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in transforming India's defence industrial base.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in achieving self-reliance in advanced defence technologies for the naval sector.
Evaluate the economic benefits and job creation potential of large-scale indigenous defence procurement.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern Indian Navy warship sailing at sea, symbolizing naval modernization.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defence equipment, a trend the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative aims to reverse.

GS3.headline: RBI MPC Maintains Repo Rate, Prioritizes Inflation Control Amid Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%, extending its pause for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects the MPC's continued cautious stance, prioritizing inflation control amidst persistent global supply chain disruptions and volatile commodity prices.
• RBI Governor stated that the focus remains on withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest that future rate cuts are unlikely until inflation shows a definitive downward trend and settles within the comfort zone.
• The move aims to maintain financial stability and anchor inflation expectations.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Monetary policy
Inflation
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for India's economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Examine the impact of global economic uncertainties on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in managing inflation and ensuring financial stability.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic indicators in the background.
GS3.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic All-Weather Tunnel in Himalayas
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) today announced the completion of a strategically vital all-weather tunnel in the challenging Himalayan terrain, significantly enhancing connectivity to remote border areas.
• This engineering marvel will ensure year-round access, crucial for military logistics, civilian movement, and economic development in the region.
• The tunnel reduces travel time and provides a safer alternative to avalanche-prone routes, especially during harsh winter months.
• It is part of India's broader strategy to upgrade border infrastructure and strengthen national security along sensitive frontiers.
• The project utilized advanced tunneling technology and overcame severe geological and climatic challenges.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: BRO (Border Roads Organisation) constructs and maintains road networks in border areas of India.
Strategic tunnels are crucial for all-weather connectivity in mountainous regions.
The Himalayas are a major mountain range in Asia.
Improved border infrastructure aids both military and civilian movement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing robust infrastructure in India's border regions, with a focus on the Himalayas.
Analyze the role of the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) in enhancing national security and economic development in challenging terrains.
Examine the engineering challenges encountered in constructing tunnels in the Himalayas and the technological solutions employed.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of improved connectivity on the lives of people residing in remote border areas.
GS3.imageDescription: An entrance to a newly constructed tunnel in a snowy mountainous region.
GS3.trivia: The Atal Tunnel, also constructed by BRO, is the world's longest highway single-tube tunnel above 10,000 feet (3,000 m).

GS3.headline: Government to Further Promote Millets and Organic Farming Through New Agricultural Reforms
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare today announced a new set of agricultural reforms aimed at boosting the cultivation of millets and promoting organic farming practices.
• These reforms include enhanced financial incentives, dedicated research and development, and market linkages for millet farmers.
• The focus on organic farming aims to reduce chemical fertilizer use, improve soil health, and cater to growing demand for sustainable produce.
• The initiatives are part of a broader strategy to diversify crop production, ensure food security, and enhance farmers' income.
• India is a major producer of millets, and the move aligns with the 'International Year of Millets' momentum.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Land reforms in India.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Millets are a group of highly nutritious and drought-resistant coarse grains (e.g., Jowar, Bajra, Ragi).
2023 was designated as the 'International Year of Millets' by the UN.
Organic farming avoids synthetic fertilizers and pesticides.
The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare is responsible for agricultural policies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of millets in ensuring India's food security, nutritional needs, and climate resilience.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of promoting organic farming practices on a large scale in India.
Examine how government policies and market interventions can support farmers in transitioning to millet cultivation and organic farming.
Evaluate the potential of agricultural diversification, including millets, in enhancing farmers' income and promoting sustainable agriculture.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting a field of millets under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: India is the largest producer of millets in the world, accounting for 41% of global production.
